paper_id: Derave et al. - 2020 - Towards a Reference Ontology for Digital Platforms

models.model_label: Digital Platform Reference Ontology (general module + one-sided + multi-sided modules)

evidence_quotes.quote: We believe this reference ontology can be a step towards a better understanding of digital platform functionality
Our ontology can help comprehend the complexity and functionality requirements of existing digital platform depending on their type.

This paper contributes to filling the lack of knowledge of digital platform functionality by proposing a method using the patterns of the Unified Foundational Ontology (UFO) [7] to create a reference ontology.

goals.justification: The ontology is explicitly framed as supporting understanding of the digital platform domain, its functionality, and conceptualization of platform types — internal domain sense-making.

evidence_quotes.quote: Apart from supporting communication between stakeholders in platform development, it can support ontology-driven development of platforms.
the reference ontology is envisioned as a common language for expressing digital platform functionality that can be used to facilitate future research and development of digital platforms.
First of all it helps both researchers and practitioners in their communication and decision making, as the ontology can be used as a common language all stakeholders can understand.

goals.justification: The paper explicitly positions the ontology as a common language to bridge stakeholders (researchers, practitioners, developers) in platform development — communication between distinct groups.

evidence_quotes.quote: First of all it helps both researchers and practitioners in their communication and decision making, as the ontology can be used as a common language all stakeholders can understand.
It can help the decision-making of developers and entrepreneurs and support ontology-driven development of new platforms.

goals.justification: The authors explicitly claim the ontology supports decision-making by researchers, practitioners, developers and entrepreneurs, separate from the intervening/implementation-instrument evidence.

evidence_quotes.quote: it can support ontology-driven development of platforms.

ontology modeling can play an essential role in areas such as database design, information systems and software development [7]. It can help the decision-making of developers and entrepreneurs and support ontology-driven development of new platforms.
an ontology can be a vital tool to accelerate the development of new digital platforms

uncertain_goals.reason: Ontology-driven development is only stated as an envisioned/future capability, not as an actual implementation; the paper explicitly notes the ontology is a first step with future work and no artifact was generated from it.
